title: Spicy Velvety Uruguayan Pepper Stir-fry
description: A low-fat-friendly monsoon dish featuring spicy uruguayan peppers and white bread cooked slowly to achieve a velvety texture.

ingredients.name: 8 slices of white bread
ingredients.amount: 6 slices for use in the recipe, 2 slices for croutons

instructions: Toast the bread slices until golden brown and slightly crispy, then cut into cubes for croutons.
Mince half of the chopped peppers. Place the other half in a large skillet over medium heat with olive oil. Once hot, add minced peppers to the pan and toss for 1-2 minutes or until fragrant.

Add the avocados and lime juice to the pepper mixture. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, for about 5-7 minutes or until the avocado begins to soften and release its oils, creating a smooth texture reminiscent of creamy vegetables.
Fold in the toasted bread cubes into the skillet. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed, then serve hot as a snack or side dish.
